channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: California residents on edge as high surf and flooding threats persist on Christmas Eve
item.description: SANTA CRUZ, Calif. (AP) — Persistent high surf and flooding threats along California’s coast had residents on high alert a day after a major storm was blamed for one man’s death and the partial collapse of a pier, which propelled three people into the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA MENDOZA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Billboard Top 10 nearly all Christmas music this week
item.description: The rest of the top 10 songs are primarily classics with a few more recent artists. Then, of course, there is Mariah Carey, but we will get to that.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, NewsNation

item.title: Biden's rationale behind Christmas present for death row killers foggy at best: expert
item.description: President Biden's rationale behind his move to commute the sentences of 37 of 40 federal inmates on death row remains unclear, according to crime data expert John Lott.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy travel day off to a rough start after American Airlines briefly grounds all flights
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — American Airlines briefly grounded flights nationwide Tuesday because of a technical problem just as the Christmas travel season kicked into overdrive and winter weather threatened more potential problems for those planning to fly or drive.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Eye drops recalled nationwide due to fungal contamination
item.description: Check your medicine cabinets: A brand of eye drops sold nationally has been recalled due to the presence of fungus, which may cause vision damage.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Seafarers move global trade
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — On a balmy December evening, four Indian crew members, eager to go explore this Mediterranean city where their container ship docked for a few hours, turned for help to Stella Maris.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL’ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A future 'SNL' star was almost cast in 'Home Alone,' but he blew his audition
item.description: Like a rusty paint can to the face, “Home Alone” left audiences in stitches upon its release in 1990. But its cast almost included a future "SNL" funnyman.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart illegally opened bank accounts for 1 million drivers, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Walmart and fintech company Branch Messenger are being sued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for allegedly forcing more than one million delivery workers to use expensive deposit accounts to access their paychecks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American Airlines nationwide ground stop lifted
item.description: American Airlines is experiencing technical issues that have affected their morning flights, according to American Airlines.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump again calls to buy Greenland after eyeing Canada and the Panama Canal
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — First it was Canada, then the Panama Canal. Now, Donald Trump again wants Greenland. The president-elect is renewing unsuccessful calls he made during his first term for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Congress looks to ban Chinese drones widely used in US: What to know about the debate
item.description: Lawmakers in Washington are seeking to ban new sales of drones from two dominant Chinese manufacturers, arguing they could be used to spy on Americans and that the low-cost models are hurting the U.S. drone industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz sues Ethics Committee in last-minute bid to block release of report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) filed a lawsuit Monday against the House Ethics Committee in a last-minute attempt to block the panel from releasing its report on him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Nordstrom to be acquired by Nordstrom family and a Mexican retail group for $6.25B
item.description: ntury-old department store Nordstrom has agreed to be acquired and taken private by Nordstrom family members and a Mexican retail group for $6.25 billion, the company said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New York City police apprehend suspect in the death of a woman found on fire in a subway car
item.description: New York City police announced Sunday they have in custody a “person of interest” in the early morning death of a woman who they believe may have fallen asleep on a stationary subway train before being intentionally lit on fire by a man she didn't know.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens to try to take back the Panama Canal. Panama's president balks at the suggestion
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Donald Trump suggested Sunday that his new administration could try to regain control of the Panama Canal that the United States “foolishly” ceded to its Central American ally, contending that shippers are charged “ridiculous” fees to pass through the vital transportation channel link
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates so Trump can't have them executed
item.description: President Joe Biden announced Monday that he is commuting the sentences of 37 of the 40 people on federal death row, converting their punishments to life imprisonment mere weeks before President-elect Donald Trump, an outspoken proponent of expanding capital punishment, takes office.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cowboys beat Tampa Bay 26-24 as Baker Mayfield and the Bucs lose control of their playoff fate
item.description: ARLINGTON, Texas (AP) — Baker Mayfield came home to Texas looking to keep his Tampa Bay Buccaneers on track for the playoffs. Instead, they'll need some help.
item.dc:creator: SCHUYLER DIXON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Abortion opponents shift focus to pills with lawsuits, proposed laws and possible federal action
item.description: Opponents of abortion are increasingly focusing on restricting access to pills, which are the most common way to end a pregnancy in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fireworks, multiple knives, pepper spray found in woman's carry-on bag at LAX
item.description: The TSA described the discovery as "shocking," even to its most tenured LAX officer, and said it was a prime example of all the things not to bring when traveling through the nation's airports.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: 174 Colorado skiers and snowboarders rescued after a lift cracks
item.description: Officials were investigating Sunday what caused a crack in a Colorado ski lift that forced the evacuation of over 170 stranded skiers and snowboarders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lindsey Vonn took all of 20 seconds to regain her former speed in comeback race at age 40
item.description: Lindsey Vonn took just a handful of gates this weekend to recover from a poor start and rediscover her former speed when she placed 14th in a super-G.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Louisiana often holds inmates past their release date, DOJ lawsuit claims
item.description: Louisiana’s prison system routinely holds people weeks and months after they have completed their sentences, the U.S. Department of Justice alleged in a lawsuit filed Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Top-ranking NYPD officer abruptly resigns amid sexual misconduct allegations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The highest-ranking uniformed officer in the New York Police Department has resigned amid allegations he demanded sex from a subordinate in exchange for opportunities to earn extra pay.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 Party City stores will stay open despite national closures
item.description: Party City stores in Hawaii will remain open, even as the national chain announced plans to close 850 locations across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Lopez, WGN 9

item.title: Blake Lively accuses 'It Ends With Us' director Justin Baldoni of harassment, smear campaign
item.description: In the complaint, Lively accuses Baldoni and the studio of embarking on a “multi-tiered plan” to damage her reputation following a meeting in which she and her husband Ryan Reynolds addressed “repeated sexual harassment and other disturbing behavior” by Baldoni and a producer on the movie.
item.dc:creator: Mallika Sen, ABC 4

item.title: Here's a look at the $100 billion in disaster relief in the government spending bill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congress is allocating more than $100 billion in emergency aid designed to address extensive damage caused by disasters after this week's scramble to find consensus on a government spending bill.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about Hanukkah and how it's celebrated around the world
item.description: Hanukkah — also spelled Chanukah or other transliterations from Hebrew — is Judaism’s “festival of lights.” On eight consecutive nightfalls, Jews gather with family and friends to light one additional candle in the menorah — a multibranched candelabra.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oregon sheriff concerned about letters asking people to track possible immigrants
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — A sheriff in Oregon says he has contacted the FBI and is increasingly concerned about a letter circulating in his rural, coastal community asking people to write down the license plate numbers of possible immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: University of California campuses resolve discrimination complaints stemming from Gaza protests
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education said Friday it has reached an agreement with the University of California system resolving complaints from Jewish and Muslim students of discrimination and harassment during protests last spring over the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WGN 9
